stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 3 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability of 86%. sensor 2 has a reliability between 67% and 80% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 3 has a reliability of 88%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all sensors simultaneously, estimated between 3.3% and 8.0%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9149 to 0.9637. Point estimate: 0.9389.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.14 × 0.33 × 0.12 = 0.0055. P(works) = 0.9945.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.14 × 0.2 × 0.12 = 0.0034. P(works) = 0.9966.
With common-cause failure (3.3%-8.0%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9149, 0.9637].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
